title: FoodGuard AI — Food Health Scanner App Frontend Implementation

requirements.summary: Implement Splash Screen with logo, app name, and tagline that auto-transitions to Home Screen
acceptanceCriteria: Splash screen is shown on initial app load for approximately 2 seconds
Displays the app logo, name 'FoodGuard AI', and a short tagline
Automatically transitions to the Home Screen after the delay

file_operations.description: Create SplashScreen component that displays the FoodGuard AI logo (frontend/public/assets/generated/foodguard-logo.dim_256x256.png), app name, tagline, and background image (frontend/public/assets/generated/splash-bg.dim_1080x1920.png). Implement useEffect with 2-second timer that navigates to home route.
Add route for SplashScreen as the default root path ('/') and wire it to navigate to '/home' after the splash delay. Ensure Home Screen is accessible at '/home'.

requirements.summary: Implement Home Screen with 'Scan Food' button, Profile navigation, and app description
acceptanceCriteria: Home screen displays a large, tappable 'Scan Food' button
Navigation link/icon to the Profile screen is accessible from Home
Brief description or tagline is visible below the app name

file_operations.description: Create HomeScreen component with hero illustration (frontend/public/assets/generated/home-hero.dim_800x600.png), app name 'FoodGuard AI', tagline/description, prominent 'Scan Food' button that navigates to '/camera', and Profile navigation icon/button that links to '/profile'. Use card-based layout and deep green primary colors.
Register '/home' route for HomeScreen component and ensure it is wired into the router configuration.

requirements.summary: Implement Camera Screen with device camera activation, capture button, and viewfinder overlay
acceptanceCriteria: Camera view is shown using the browser MediaDevices API
A 'Capture' button takes a snapshot of the current frame
A viewfinder/frame overlay guides the user to position the food item
User can cancel and return to the Home Screen

file_operations.description: Create CameraScreen component that uses the camera component's useCamera hook to activate the device camera with facingMode: 'environment'. Display video preview with viewfinder overlay/frame, 'Capture' button to take snapshot, and 'Cancel' button to navigate back to '/home'. On capture, navigate to '/food-input' with captured image data. Verify the camera component's usage instructions before implementing. Ensure camera preview has stable dimensions (aspect-ratio wrapper or fixed min-height) and handle error states with clear messages. Disable capture button until camera is fully initialized.
Register '/camera' route for CameraScreen component.

requirements.summary: Implement packaged food ingredient scanner with OCR simulation, harmful ingredient detection, and warning badges
acceptanceCriteria: A text area or input field is presented after 'label scan' mode is selected, accepting ingredient text
The system detects at least: high sugar/sugar variants, MSG/monosodium glutamate, artificial color names (Red 40, Yellow 5, Blue 1, etc.), and common preservatives (sodium benzoate, BHA, BHT, TBHQ)
Each detected harmful ingredient is shown as a colored warning badge
A summary warning message is shown if any harmful ingredient is detected
Backend stores the list of harmful ingredient keywords used for detection

file_operations.description: Create IngredientScanScreen component with a large text area for user to paste/type ingredient text extracted from label. Parse the text into an array of ingredient strings (split by commas/newlines). Include a 'Scan Ingredients' button that calls backend.scanIngredients() with the parsed array. Display loading state during scan. On completion, show ProcessedIngredient results with warning badges for harmful ingredients (isHarmful: true), and a summary warning message if harmfulCount > 0. Provide 'Scan Another' button to reset and 'View Details' button to navigate to '/result' if a food name can be inferred.
Register '/ingredient-scan' route for IngredientScanScreen component.

Add mode selection UI (e.g., tabs or toggle) to switch between 'Food Item' and 'Ingredient Label' modes. In 'Food Item' mode, navigate to '/food-input' after capture. In 'Ingredient Label' mode, navigate to '/ingredient-scan' after capture.

requirements.summary: Implement Result Screen displaying food name, calories, sugar/fat levels, circular health score gauge, status badge, and harmful ingredient warnings
acceptanceCriteria: Result screen renders after a scan is completed
Displays food name, estimated calories, sugar level, and fat level
A circular gauge or progress arc displays the health score (0–100)
Status badge is green for Safe (80–100), yellow for Moderate (50–79), red for Harmful (<50)
Harmful ingredient warnings are listed with warning icons when present
A 'Scan Again' button returns the user to the Camera Screen

file_operations.description: Create ResultScreen component that receives FoodAnalysisResult from navigation state or fetches via backend.analyzeFood(). Display food name, calories, sugarLevel, fatLevel using card-based layout. Render a circular gauge/progress arc component for healthScore (0–100). Display status badge with color-coding: green for 'safe' (80–100), yellow for 'moderate' (50–79), red for 'harmful' (<50). If harmful ingredients are present (from ingredient scan context), list them with warning icons. Include 'Scan Again' button navigating to '/camera' and 'View Profile' button navigating to '/profile'.

Create reusable HealthScoreGauge component that accepts a score (0–100) prop and renders a circular SVG gauge or progress arc with color gradient based on score range (green 80–100, yellow 50–79, red <50). Display numeric score in center.
Register '/result' route for ResultScreen component.

Create FoodInputScreen component with a text input field for user to enter or confirm the food item name (simulate OCR/image recognition step). On submit, call backend.analyzeFood(foodName) and navigate to '/result' with the returned FoodAnalysisResult in navigation state.
Register '/food-input' route for FoodInputScreen component.

requirements.summary: Implement Profile Screen for entering and saving age, weight, and health conditions
acceptanceCriteria: Profile screen has input fields for age (number), weight (number, kg), and a multi-select or checkbox list of health conditions
A 'Save Profile' button persists the data to the backend
Saved profile data is loaded and pre-filled when the user revisits the Profile screen
Backend stores the user profile with age, weight, and conditions array

file_operations.description: Create ProfileScreen component with form inputs: age (number input), weight (number input, label 'kg'), and multi-select checkboxes for health conditions (Diabetes, High Blood Pressure, High Cholesterol, Heart Disease, Obesity, None). On mount, fetch existing profile via backend.getUserProfile() and pre-fill form fields. On 'Save Profile' button click, call backend.saveUserProfile() with UserProfile object containing age, weight, and healthConditions array. Show success/error feedback. Include 'Back to Home' navigation button.
Register '/profile' route for ProfileScreen component.

requirements.summary: Implement personalized health warnings on Result Screen based on saved user profile and food analysis
acceptanceCriteria: Backend exposes a function that accepts a food result and a user profile and returns a list of personalized warning strings
At least 4 condition-food interactions are handled: Diabetes + high sugar, High Blood Pressure + high sodium/preservatives, Heart Disease + high fat, Obesity + high calories
Personalized warnings are displayed as a distinct section on the Result Screen with an alert icon
No warnings are shown if the user has no saved health conditions or if the food poses no relevant risk

file_operations.description: After loading FoodAnalysisResult, fetch personalized warnings by calling backend.getHealthWarnings(foodName). Display returned warnings in a distinct 'Personalized Health Warnings' section with alert icons. Only show this section if warnings array is non-empty. Style warnings with yellow/orange alert styling.

requirements.summary: Apply cohesive visual design with deep green and white palette, modern typography, card layouts, and smooth transitions
acceptanceCriteria: All screens share a consistent color palette (deep green primary, white backgrounds, red/yellow/green for status indicators)
Typography is consistent using a single modern sans-serif font scale
Cards are used to group content sections on Result and Profile screens
Navigation between screens uses smooth CSS transitions
The app is responsive and optimized for a mobile viewport (max-width ~430px)

file_operations.description: Define global CSS variables for deep green primary color (#1B5E20 or similar), white backgrounds, status colors (green #4CAF50, yellow #FFC107, red #F44336), and modern sans-serif font stack (e.g., 'Inter', system-ui). Apply base typography styles, card styling classes (.card with border-radius, shadow, padding), and smooth page transition classes. Set body max-width for mobile optimization.

Extend Tailwind theme to include custom colors for 'primary' (deep green), 'status-safe' (green), 'status-moderate' (yellow), 'status-harmful' (red), and 'card' (white with subtle shadow). Configure responsive breakpoints optimized for mobile viewports (max-width ~430px).

Create Layout component wrapper that applies consistent header/navigation styling, mobile-optimized container (max-width), and card-based section wrappers. Use this component to wrap all page routes for consistent design.
Wrap all routes with Layout component to enforce consistent design and navigation structure across all screens.
